title: QA: empty bash command absent-key path returns target="" allowing default_allow="" to fire
description:
## Severity
Major
## Dimension
security
## Location
src/permissions.rs:274-302, 215-226
## Violation
`extract_target` for `"bash"` returns an empty target string when the
`command` key is absent entirely:
```rust
match (cmd, tool_args.get("command")) {
    (Some(s), _) => Some(Ok(("Bash".to_string(), s.to_string()))),
    (None, Some(_)) => Some(Err("Bash".to_string())),
    (None, None) => Some(Ok(("Bash".to_string(), String::new()))), // key absent
}
```
That empty target then flows into the `default_allow` loop:
```rust
for pattern in &self.default_allow {
    if Self::glob_matches(pattern, &target) {
        return CheckResult::Allowed;
    }
}
```
The pin test `b2_empty_glob_matches_only_empty_target` confirms a
`default_allow = [""]` rule **allows** a bash call with no `command`
key. This is a hostile path: a model can submit `{"tool":"bash"}` with
no args to slip past empty-string allow patterns and reach tool
execution. The model never needs the command key — the downstream tool
dispatcher decides what to run.
## Mandated refactor
Treat a missing `command` key as malformed: return
`Some(Err("Bash".to_string()))` from `extract_target` so the
`Denied(...)` path at line 145 fires. A bash invocation without a
command is never legitimate — there is nothing to execute. Add a test
that pins this denial.
## Filed from QA review dispatch
